title: Fix hardcoded (0,0) spans in compiler ValueCellDecl output
analysis: The compiler's compile_structure function at crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs creates ValueCellDecl entries with hardcoded SourceSpan::new(0, 0) instead of propagating spans from the parsed AST. This affects three locations: (1) auto params at line 718, (2) regular params at line 731, and (3) let bindings at line 753. Constraint spans are already correctly propagated at line 778 via `span: constraint.span`, and an existing test `compiled_constraint_spans_match_parsed_spans` in boundary1_consumer.rs already validates this. The fix is straightforward: replace SourceSpan::new(0, 0) with param.span or let_decl.span respectively. The parsed AST types (ParamDecl, LetDecl) all have `pub span: SourceSpan` fields populated by the parser. The test-support builders (reify-test-support/src/builders.rs) also use SourceSpan::new(0, 0) in param() and let_binding() methods, but those are test infrastructure not production code and are out of scope for this task (module: crates/reify-test-support). Since the existing bracket_compiled_module() fixture and its consumers don't assert on ValueCellDecl spans, the builder change is not needed to make tests pass.

steps.description: Write failing test: compiled param ValueCellDecl spans must match parsed ParamDecl spans (not 0,0). In boundary1_consumer.rs, add test `compiled_param_spans_match_parsed_spans` that parses bracket_source(), compiles it, extracts the 5 param ValueCellDecls, and asserts each span != SourceSpan::new(0, 0) and matches the corresponding ParamDecl.span from the parsed AST. Follow the same pattern as the existing `compiled_constraint_spans_match_parsed_spans` test. This test will fail because lines 718 and 731 hardcode SourceSpan::new(0, 0).

Write failing test: compiled let ValueCellDecl spans must match parsed LetDecl spans (not 0,0). In boundary1_consumer.rs, add test `compiled_let_spans_match_parsed_spans` that parses bracket_source(), compiles it, finds the volume let binding ValueCellDecl (the body let is skipped by the compiler as a geometry-producing let), and asserts its span != SourceSpan::new(0, 0) and matches the parsed LetDecl.span for the volume binding. This test will fail because line 753 hardcodes SourceSpan::new(0, 0).

Write failing test: auto param ValueCellDecl spans must be non-zero and match parsed span. In boundary2_producer.rs, add test `compiled_auto_param_span_not_zero` that parses `structure S { param x: Scalar = auto }`, compiles it, and asserts the auto param ValueCellDecl.span != SourceSpan::new(0, 0). This test will fail because line 718 hardcodes SourceSpan::new(0, 0) for auto params.

Fix the three hardcoded spans in compile_structure (lib.rs): (1) line 718 auto param: change `span: SourceSpan::new(0, 0)` to `span: param.span`, (2) line 731 regular param: change `span: SourceSpan::new(0, 0)` to `span: param.span`, (3) line 753 let binding: change `span: SourceSpan::new(0, 0)` to `span: let_decl.span`. All three tests from steps 1-3 should now pass.

design_decisions.decision: Use param.span and let_decl.span (the declaration-level span) rather than individual sub-expression spans
design_decisions.rationale: The ValueCellDecl.span should encompass the entire declaration (e.g., 'param width: Scalar = 80mm'), not just a sub-part. The parsed AST already has declaration-level spans on ParamDecl.span, LetDecl.span, and ConstraintDecl.span. The constraint path already uses constraint.span correctly, so params and lets should follow the same pattern for consistency.

design_decisions.decision: Add tests in both boundary1_consumer.rs and boundary2_producer.rs
design_decisions.rationale: Param and let span tests use parse-then-compile (consumer pattern, matching the existing constraint span test in boundary1). The auto param test goes in boundary2 (producer-side) since it mirrors the existing compile_auto_param test structure there which also parses inline source strings.

design_decisions.decision: Do not modify test-support builders (reify-test-support/src/builders.rs) in this task
design_decisions.rationale: The builders' param() and let_binding() methods also use SourceSpan::new(0, 0), but they are test infrastructure outside the crates/reify-compiler module scope. No existing tests assert on builder-produced spans, so there's no breakage. Updating builders would be a separate task touching crates/reify-test-support.

reuse.how: Reuse the same pattern as compiled_constraint_spans_match_parsed_spans: parse bracket_source(), compile, extract parsed spans from AST by filtering MemberDecl variants, compare with compiled output spans.
Follow the same inline parse-from-source pattern for the auto param span test, adding a span assertion to verify it's non-zero.
The constraint span test already establishes the pattern of asserting both != SourceSpan::new(0, 0) and == parsed span. Reuse this dual-assertion approach for param and let span tests.
